name: Wire Rope Sling Load and Rigging Angle Calculator — ASME B30 Sling Tension and Utilization Check
subtitle: Lifting / Rigging / Wire Rope Slings — ASME B30.9-2025, ASME B30.26-2026, ASME P30.1, OSHA Safe Sling Use guidance, ISO 80000, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3 GUM, ISO 9001:2015 §8.5.1

scope.boundaryWarning: Engineering decision support only — verify before use.

inputs.name: Load Mass to be Lifted
inputs.hint: Mass of the load to be lifted. Include all attachments, rigging hardware, and any ancillary equipment suspended below the crane hook.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty: ±1.5% typical for weighed loads; ±5% for estimated loads

inputs.name: Local Gravitational Acceleration (g)
inputs.hint: Standard gravity = 9.80665 m/s². Variations: 9.764 (Equator) to 9.834 (Poles). Use local value for precision lifts.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 80000-3:2019 — Standard gravity gₙ = 9.80665 m/s²; local variation ±0.35%

inputs.name: Dynamic Amplification Factor (DAF)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for dynamic effects during lift. ASME B30.9: 1.0 for static/controlled; 1.25 for normal crane lifts; 1.5 for rough terrain; 2.0 for sudden release or shock loading.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.2 — Dynamic factor uncertainty: ±5% for normal lifts, ±10% for shock loading

inputs.name: Rigging Configuration / Hitch Type
inputs.hint: Wire rope sling configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 3 defines configuration factors. Bridle: legs share load. Basket: sling wraps around load. Choker: sling passes through itself around load.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Configuration definitions, zero configuration uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Sling Legs Installed
inputs.hint: Total number of sling legs installed in the rigging arrangement. Maximum 4 legs per ASME B30.9.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Discrete count variable — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Active Load-Bearing Legs
inputs.hint: Number of legs actually carrying the load. For 3-leg bridle with rigid load: often only 2 legs carry load due to load distribution. ASME B30.9: do not assume equal load sharing without load-balancing device.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution: discrete assessment, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load-Balancing Device Present
inputs.hint: Is a load-balancing device (e.g., equalizer beam, spreader bar, load cell feedback) used to ensure equal load distribution among all sling legs?
inputs.uncertainty_source: Boolean — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sling Angle from Horizontal (θ)
inputs.hint: Angle between the sling leg and the horizontal plane. ASME B30.9: minimum 30° from horizontal. OSHA: minimum 30° (30° = 2x tension factor). 60° = 1.155x tension factor; 90° = 1.0x tension factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty: ±2° typical for field measurement; ±1° for digital inclinometer

inputs.name: Sling Length (L_s)
inputs.hint: Total length of the sling from master link to hook or end fitting. Used for vertical rise and horizontal offset calculation if not directly provided.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Length measurement tolerance class m: ±0.5%

inputs.name: Vertical Rise from Load to Hook (H)
inputs.hint: Vertical distance from the load attachment point to the crane hook. Used to verify sling angle if sling_length_m and horizontal_offset_m are provided.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Height measurement tolerance: ±1%

inputs.name: Horizontal Offset from Hook to Load (D)
inputs.hint: Horizontal distance from the crane hook centerline to the load attachment point. Combined with vertical_rise_m to verify sling angle. Zero for centered lifts.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Distance measurement tolerance: ±1%

inputs.name: Wire Rope Sling WLL — Vertical / Basket (per leg)
inputs.hint: Manufacturer-rated Working Load Limit for this sling in vertical configuration. ASME B30.9 Table 4: WLL shall be marked on sling tag or nameplate. Includes termination efficiency.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.1.2 — Manufacturer WLL rating uncertainty: ±3% (includes material, manufacturing, and test variability)

inputs.name: Hitch Configuration Capacity Factor
inputs.hint: Factor applied to vertical WLL for hitch type: Single Vertical = 1.0, 2-Leg Bridle = 1.0 per leg, Single Basket = 2.0, Double Basket = 2.0 per leg (depends on D/d ratio), Choker = 0.75–0.80.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 Table 3 — Hitch capacity factors, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: D/d Ratio (Bend Radius / Rope Diameter)
inputs.hint: Ratio of the diameter of curvature (D) of the pin/hook/shackle over the nominal rope diameter (d). ASME B30.26 Table 1: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles; D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. Lower D/d reduces sling capacity.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §8.3.1 — D/d measurement uncertainty: ±5% field estimate

inputs.name: Minimum Required D/d Ratio per Standard
inputs.hint: Minimum D/d ratio required by the applicable standard. ASME B30.26: D/d ≥ 5 for shackles, D/d ≥ 2 for hooks. EN 1677: D/d ≥ 5. Lower values require mandatory reduction factor.
inputs.uncertainty_source: Standard specification — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: D/d Reduction Factor (f_dd)
inputs.hint: Reduction factor for sling WLL based on D/d ratio. D/d ≥ minimum_required: f_dd = 1.0. D/d < minimum_required: apply reduction. ASME B30.9: f_dd = actual_Dd / minimum_Dd for basket hitches.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.3 — D/d reduction factor, ±1% for interpolation

inputs.name: Termination Efficiency Factor (η_ter)
inputs.hint: Efficiency of sling end terminations (swaged sleeves, hand-spliced eyes, mechanical splices). Swaged sleeve Flemish eye: 0.95–1.0. Hand-tucked splice (6 tucks): 0.80–0.85. Mechanical wedge socket: 0.90. Mechanical clamped: 0.75.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.4 — Termination efficiency: ±2% for swaged, ±5% for hand-spliced

inputs.name: Edge Protection Factor (f_edge)
inputs.hint: Factor for edge contact/corner protection. 1.0 = proper edge protection (corner protectors, saddles); 0.9 = rounded edges with padding; 0.8 = soft metal edge contact; 0.7 = sharp edges without protection. ASME B30.9 §8.5: sharp edges must be padded.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §8.5 — Edge protection: ±3% for good protection, ±10% for marginal

inputs.name: Weakest Rigging Hardware WLL
inputs.hint: Working Load Limit of the weakest component in the rigging assembly (shackle, master link, hook, ring). ASME B30.26: all rigging hardware shall be rated and marked. The system is only as strong as its weakest component.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.26-2026 §5.1 — Hardware WLL rating uncertainty: ±3%

inputs.name: Load Distribution Factor (f_dist)
inputs.hint: Factor accounting for unequal load distribution among legs. 1.0 = equal distribution (load-balancing device present and verified); 0.9 = good distribution (rigid load, symmetric geometry); 0.8 = moderate distribution; 0.67 = conservative (no load-balancing, 3+ legs, ASME B30.9 default).
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.1.3 — Load distribution factor: conservative estimate ±2%

inputs.name: Manufacturer Tag / Nameplate Present
inputs.hint: Is the manufacturer tag (with WLL, grade, serial number, manufacturer ID) present and legible on each sling as required by ASME B30.9 §5.1.2?
inputs.uncertainty_source: Boolean — zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sling Inspection Status
inputs.hint: Most recent inspection status per ASME B30.9 §6: Periodic inspection (annual minimum, monthly for heavy use). PASS = no defects found. CONDITIONAL = minor wear/damage requiring engineering review. REJECTED = failed inspection, do not use.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §6 — Inspection status: zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Load Mass Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in load mass. Weighed loads: ±1.5%. Calculated from known density/dimensions: ±5%. Estimated: ±10% or more.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME P30.1 §4.3.1 — Load weight uncertainty categories

inputs.name: Sling Angle Measurement Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Uncertainty in sling angle measurement. Digital inclinometer: ±0.5°. Protractor: ±1°. Visual estimation: ±5°.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.1 — Angle measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Sling Capacity Uncertainty
inputs.hint: Percentage uncertainty in sling rated capacity. Manufacturer-rated: ±3%. Derated/aging sling: ±5–10%.
inputs.uncertainty_source: ASME B30.9-2025 §5.1.2 — Capacity rating uncertainty

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling angle out of range (V1)
fmea.effect: Sling angle θ < 30° or > 90° — invalid geometry per ASME B30.9
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: active leg count invalid (V2)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: load mass zero or negative (V3)
fmea.effect: Load weight undefined — lift not possible
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Non-conservative dynamic factor — may underestimate load
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: no load-balancing device with ≥3 legs (V5)
fmea.effect: Unequal load distribution with multi-leg bridle, conservative assessment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic WARNING on violation

fmea.effect: No effective capacity — sling or hardware WLL invalid
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Input violation: sling WLL not positive (V7)
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Sling identification not present — ASME B30.9 violation
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation
Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Design tension exceeds effective WLL — risk of sling failure and dropped load
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.4 — UC threshold monitoring with critical alert

fmea.effect: High utilization — reduced safety margin for dynamic events
High tension factor increases sling load significantly
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §9.2.4 — Angle warning with recommendation

fmea.effect: Potential hidden damage or wear affecting capacity
fmea.control_measure: ASME B30.9-2025 §6.4 — Conditional use requires engineering sign-off

fmea.effect: Reduced sling capacity due to tight bend radius
